<!-- Tip: Use /create-instructions in chat to generate content with agent assistance -->

Provide project context and coding guidelines that AI should follow when generating code, answering questions, or reviewing changes.

Never use emojis in code comments or documentation. Use clear, concise language. Follow the existing code style and conventions used in the project. When in doubt, refer to the project's README and design documents for guidance on architecture and best practices.

Always choose clarity and consitency over cleverness. Write code that is easy to read and understand, even if it means being more verbose. Avoid complex one-liners or overly abstracted code that may be difficult for other developers to follow.

When generating code, ensure that it is well-structured, modular, and adheres to the principles of clean code. Use meaningful variable and function names, and include comments where necessary to explain the purpose and logic of the code.

When answering questions or reviewing changes, provide detailed explanations and reasoning behind your suggestions. Consider the implications of your recommendations on the overall system design and architecture, and how they align with the project's goals and requirements.
